title: Agonandra goldbergiana
intro: Agonandra goldbergiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Opiliaceae, native to Central and South America, and is also known as the "Goldberg's Agonandra". The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which have been found to help alleviate symptoms of various diseases, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Agonandra goldbergiana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Agonandra goldbergiana belongs to the family Annonaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. The genus Agonandra is a small group of evergreen trees and shrubs native to Central and South America. This species, Agonandra goldbergiana, is a tree with glossy leaves and white, fragrant flowers. The plant is often used in ornamental gardens due to its unique appearance. The classification of Agonandra goldbergiana places it within the broader family Annonaceae, alongside other related species.
